title: From Matroids to Moduli Spaces
speaker: Lukas Kühne
abstract:
Abstract: After a gentle introduction to matroids, I will present parts of a new OSCAR software module for matroids through several examples. I will focus on computing the moduli space of a matroid which is the space of all arrangements of hyperplanes with that matroid as their intersection lattice.
In the second part, I will discuss applications of this module in the fields of particle physics, algebraic geometry, and theoretical computer science.

proof_stages.label: Classical realization spaces of matroids
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines linear realizations of matroids, packages all realizations as matroid strata in Grassmannians modulo the column-scaling torus, and recalls universality and singularity phenomena for these spaces.

proof_stages.label: Computing realization schemes in OSCAR
proof_stages.summary: An OSCAR implementation computes realization-space equations over the integers, tests realizability in specified characteristics or finite fields, extracts sample realizations, and searches databases for phenomena such as singular spaces.

proof_stages.label: Lambda operators on line arrangements
proof_stages.summary: The speaker introduces Roulleau's operators that send a line arrangement to a new arrangement determined by multiplicity conditions on intersection points, emphasizing that the operation depends on geometric realization data rather than only on the underlying matroid.

proof_stages.label: Polygon matroids and elliptic modular surfaces
proof_stages.summary: For deformed regular k-gon matroids, the realization spaces carry lambda dynamics and are related by a commutative diagram to elliptic modular surfaces, yielding birational singular models and connecting to elliptic modular curves.

proof_stages.label: Multilinear representations by block matrices
proof_stages.summary: Classical matrices are generalized to block matrices, or c-arrangements of higher-codimensional subspaces, whose block-minor ranks recover the matroid rank function and can realize matroids not realizable over fields.

proof_stages.label: Partition-representable and entropic matroids
proof_stages.summary: Matoušek's partition representations are introduced, linear and multilinear representations are shown to give such partitions, and for connected matroids this is identified with entropicity of the rank function.

proof_stages.label: Undecidability via generalized Dowling geometries
proof_stages.summary: The speaker proves undecidability of entropic, partition, and multilinear representability by reducing the uniform word problem for finite groups to entropicity of generalized Dowling-type matroids, with conditional-independence implication undecidability as a corollary.
